title: QA Area Quantization PASS: mod-9 quadrea spectrum {0,1,2,4,5,7,8} — forbidden {3,6}
_fixture_purpose: Canonical witness for the mod-9 quadrea spectrum. The Q(sqrt(5)) norm form f(b,e)=b^2+be-e^2 over Z/9Z takes exactly the values {0,1,2,4,5,7,8} and never takes {3,6}. Proof: f(b,e) ≡ 0 mod 3 iff both b≡0 and e≡0 mod 3 (since for b,e not both divisible by 3, checking all residue class pairs shows f ≢ 0 mod 3). Therefore if f≡0 mod 3 then 3|b and 3|e, giving b=3b', e=3e', f=9(b'^2+b'e'-e'^2) ≡ 0 mod 9. So f mod 9 is never in {3,6} = {n : 3|n but 9∤n}. This is the first family_extension of QA_CORE_SPEC.v1, demonstrating kernel inheritance with a concrete number-theoretic result.

quadrea_claim.forbidden_reason: 3 is inert in Z[phi] (Legendre symbol (5/3)=(2/3)=-1). For b,e not both divisible by 3, f(b,e) mod 3 takes only non-zero values {1,2} — never 0. Therefore f(b,e) ≡ 0 mod 3 iff 3|b and 3|e, which forces f ≡ 0 mod 9. The values 3 and 6 (i.e., 0 mod 3 but not mod 9) are structurally unreachable.

generators.name: sigma
generators.action: e → e + 1 (mod 9); updates d = b+e mod 9, a = e+d mod 9

validation_checks.details: kernel types {OUT_OF_BOUNDS,PARITY,INVARIANT_BREAK,ZERO_DENOMINATOR} all present; adds QUADREA_MISMATCH, FORBIDDEN_QUADREA_INCORRECT

validation_checks.description: quadrea_claim.spectrum matches computed f(b,e) mod 9 for all b,e
